ddls:
CREATE TABLE "abalone" (
  "abalone_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each abalone specimen */,
  "rings" INTEGER /* Number of rings on the abalone shell */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the abalone (M, F, or I) */,
  "length" REAL /* Length of the abalone */,
  "diameter" REAL /* Diameter of the abalone */,
  "height" REAL /* Height of the abalone */,
  "whole_weight" REAL /* Whole weight of the abalone */,
  "shucked_weight" REAL /* Weight of the abalone after shucking */,
  "viscera_weight" REAL /* Weight of the abalone viscera */,
  "shell_weight" REAL /* Weight of the abalone shell */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source for the abalone specimen */,
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data collection for the abalone specimen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("abalone_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_abalone_collection_id FOREIGN KEY ("collection_id") REFERENCES data_collections ("collection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_abalone_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Name of the researcher */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution the researcher is affiliated with */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Researcher's area of specialization */,
  "experience" INTEGER /* Researcher's years of experience */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_collections" (
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data collection */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher who collected the data */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the data was collected */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source for the collection */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used for collecting the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_collections_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_collections_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "abalone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the abalone specimen being measured */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date the measurement was taken */,
  "length" REAL /* Length of the abalone */,
  "diameter" REAL /* Diameter of the abalone */,
  "height" REAL /* Height of the abalone */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the abalone */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement taken */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_abalone_id FOREIGN KEY ("abalone_id") REFERENCES abalone ("abalone_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each abalone specimen
